title: Marine Expeditionary Unit Concludes 10-Month Southcom Deployment
description: Marines and sailors assigned to the 22nd Marine Expeditionary Unit returned to Camp Lejeune, N.C., after a 10-month U.S. Southern Command deployment.

body.text: Marines and sailors assigned to the 22nd Marine Expeditionary Unit began returning home to Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune, North Carolina, in waves June 1 after completing a nearly 10‑month deployment in support of Operation Southern Spear and U.S. Southern Command's priorities of countering illicit threats, strengthening regional partnerships and protecting the homeland.

A Marine Corps MV-22B Osprey tiltrotor aircraft assigned to Marine Medium Tiltrotor Squadron 263, 22nd Marine Expeditionary Unit, takes off from the amphibious assault ship USS Iwo Jima during flight operations while underway in the Caribbean, May 13, 2026. Joint forces are deployed to the Caribbean in support of the U.S. Southern Command mission to disrupt illicit drug trafficking and protect the homeland.
